DECLARATION OF KIM SPENCER IN REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF TOUPS, JILL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JILL TOUPS'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. THE PARTIES AGREE THAT THE FACTS OF THIS CASE ARE UNDISPUTED, INCLUDING THE AMOUNT IN DISPUTE ($83,000). PLAINTIFF HAS MET ITS BURDEN OF PROVING THE ELEMENTS OF UNJUST ENRICHMENTS OF UNJUST ENRICHMENT AND INVOLUNTARY TRUST. WHILE DEFENDANT MAY BE ENTITLED TO ATTORNEY FEES UNDER THE "TORT OF ANOTHER" DOCTRINE, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE THIS ON A MSA/MSJ BECAUSE THE SPECIFIC AMOUNT OF FEES IS IN DISPUTE. THIS SHOULD BE BROUGHT IN A SEPARATE MOTION FOR FEES AND COSTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT ISSUED TO PLAINTIFF TOUPS, JILL

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JILL TOUPS'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION.
Appearances: Russell M. Davis of the Law Offices of Russell Davis (409.5627) for plaintiff and moving party; Jeffrey A. Chen of the Law Offices of Jeffrey A. Chen (348.6269) for defendant and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: plaintiff's motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the First and Second causes of action.
The parties agree that the facts of this case are undisputed, including the amount in dispute ($83,000).
Plaintiff has met its burden of proving the elements of unjust enrichments of unjust enrichment and involuntary trust. While Defendant may be entitled to attorney fees under the "tort of another" doctrine, the court cannot determine this on a MSA/MSJ because the specific amount of fees is in dispute. This should be brought in a separate motion for fees and costs. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
